name: Olive Leaf Extract
summary: Olive leaf extract reduces blood pressure comparably to low-dose captopril.
description: Olive leaf extract lowers blood pressure with effects on vascular tone and endothelial function; human comparisons suggest clinically relevant antihypertensive activity.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v9.6: Evidence is promising but not strong enough for high-priority placement.
claimMap.notes: v9.6: Evidence is promising but not strong enough for high-priority placement.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Olive leaf extract doses vary; 500–1000 mg/day studied in cardiometabolic trials

sources.title: PubMed PMID 21443487. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 21443487. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: known hypersensitivity to olive leaf or olive products
antihypertensive medications such as beta-blockers or ACE inhibitors or calcium channel blockers due to additive blood-pressure-lowering effect
diabetes medication or insulin due to enhanced blood sugar lowering effect and hypoglycemia risk
anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications due to increased bleeding risk
pregnancy or breastfeeding due to insufficient safety data
